steps.title: Convert fractional exponents to radical notation
Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• $\frac{\sqrt{y}}{\sqrt[4]{x^3}}$. This gives $y^{1/2} / x^{3/4}$, but we need $y^{3/2} / x^{13/4}$. Eliminated.
  • $\frac{y\sqrt{y}}{\sqrt[4]{x^3}}$. The numerator is correct, but the denominator is $x^{3/4}$, not $x^{13/4}$. Eliminated.
  • $\frac{y\sqrt{y}}{x^2\sqrt{x}}$. The denominator is $x^{5/2}$, but we need $x^{13/4}$. Eliminated.
  • The matching choice: $\frac{y\sqrt{y}}{x^3 \sqrt[4]{x}}$. The denominator is $x^3 \cdot x^{1/4} = x^{13/4}$, which matches exactly. D is correct.
